classification_actions._doc_schema: Per-classification routing. Tags are NOT configurable here — they are hard-coded in routing.py by design (always 'replied' + classification name + conditional 'low_confidence'/'ambiguous_contact_match'/'auto_created_from_reply'). Adding new tag logic requires a code change, not a config change. The 'unsubscribe' entry's auto_send and min_confidence values are present for schema consistency but are IGNORED at runtime — see §5.4 carve-out (unsubscribe always honors regardless of confidence).

unsubscribe._doc: Static template. CAN-SPAM: bypasses confidence gate. See §5.4.

interested._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews. See §5.3.

not_now._doc: Date extraction populates contract_end_date when parseable.

info_request._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews.

objection._doc: WEEK 4-6 OPTIMIZATION: flip auto_send true after 20-30 clean reviews.

qualification_rubric:
Score this account 1-10 as a prospect for a premium commercial cleaning service in the Twin Cities metro area.

THE CLIENT'S ICP:
- THREE deal types:
  - "Velocity": small offices, up to 2hr clean, 1-2 cleans/week, $500-$1,000/mo ($125-$150/visit), 50%+ margins, 1-week close cycle. Think residential-style teams doing 3-5 stops per night in clusters.
  - "Mid-Market": 20K-50K sqft offices, 3-5 days/week, $3,000-$15,000+/mo, 20-30% margins, 1-3 month close cycle (discovery → walkthrough → RFP → followups + legal → close).
  - "Enterprise": Large campus accounts (Cambria, 3M, General Mills, Best Buy, Target), $20,000+/mo, 30%+ margins. Longer cycle but high value. Do NOT cap at mid-market — enterprise is explicitly in scope.

- Best customers: warehouse/logistics-style locations, 20K-50K sqft, $1,500-$3,000/site at 3 days/week. Standardized cleaning with auto-scrubber maintenance. Specialized equipment requirement filters out low-cost competitors.

- Will serve ANY building type EXCEPT: restaurants, hospitality (hotels, bowling alleys, wedding venues), retail, car dealerships, dentist offices, hospitals.

- Will serve medical practitioner offices (NOT hospitals, NOT dentist offices).

- Geography: 45-minute radius of Minneapolis/St. Paul.

- Government rules: NO state, federal, or county government (Shawn already gets RFP notifications). YES to city-level small municipalities (Eden Prairie, Shakopee, Corcoran, etc.)

- National big box retailers: EXCLUDE. They use national contractors, have 2-hour pre-open windows, and are a staffing nightmare.

- Procurement titles: EXCLUDE from targeting. They focus on cost reduction and don't feel the operational pain.

QUALIFICATION THRESHOLDS (lighter filters at launch per Shawn's explicit request):
- Auto-disqualify ONLY when ALL THREE conditions are met: monthly < $500 AND per-visit < $100 AND margin < 10%. Per-visit under $100 ALONE is NOT auto-disqualify — high-frequency low-per-visit can still be profitable.
- Gray zone: $1,000-$2,000/month, ~$100/visit, < 15% margin
- Qualified: $3,000-$6,000/month, ~$125/visit, 15%+ margin
- Premium: $7,500-$15,000/month, ~$350/visit, 20%+ margin
- High-value: $20,000+/month, ~$450/visit, 30%+ margin

HIGH SCORE SIGNALS (7-10):
- Multi-tenant office building 20K-50K sqft (sweet spot)
- Warehouse, logistics center, or large commercial space with auto-scrubber surfaces
- Medical practitioner office (not hospital, not dentist)
- Property management company with multiple buildings
- Recent facility manager job posting (hiring = transition = vendor evaluation)
- Recent commercial lease signing (new tenant = new cleaning needs) — THIS IS THE #1 SIGNAL per Shawn
- Building permits indicating renovation/expansion
- Negative reviews about current cleaning vendor or facility cleanliness
- Winter season (highest vendor turnover in Twin Cities due to weather)
- Geographic cluster near existing velocity accounts (route density for team leads)
- Enterprise campus (Cambria, 3M, General Mills, Best Buy, Target)
- Bombora or similar showing janitorial topic interest at company level
- Company showing interest in major contractors like ABM or JLL (shopping signal)

MEDIUM SCORE SIGNALS (4-6):
- Small office under 5,000 sqft with no strong intent signals (potential velocity but uncertain)
- Industrial/manufacturing (may need cleaning but less predictable)
- No negative signals but no positive intent signals either
- City-level municipality (valid target but longer procurement cycle)
- Building between 5K-20K sqft (between velocity and mid-market sweet spots)

LOW SCORE SIGNALS (1-3):
- Restaurant, hotel, hospitality venue → score 1 (auto-reject)
- Retail store → score 1 (auto-reject)
- Car dealership → score 1 (auto-reject)
- Dentist office → score 1 (auto-reject, NEW)
- Hospital or large healthcare system → score 1 (auto-reject)
- National big box retailer (PetSmart stores, Target stores, etc.) → score 1 (auto-reject, NEW)
- State/federal/county government building → score 1 (auto-reject for cold email, NEW)
- Residential property → score 1 (auto-reject)
- Located outside 45-minute radius → score 1 (auto-reject)
- Very small business with no facility needs → score 2
- Recently signed a cleaning contract (not in market) → score 3
- Procurement-only contacts with no facility operations role → score 2 (NEW)

Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text:
{"score": X, "reasoning": "...", "deal_type": "velocity|mid_market|enterprise|disqualified"}
_qualify_pipeline_stage_doc: GHL stage 'Walkthrough Scheduled' — discovered via scripts/provision_qualification_setup.py on 2026-05-20.
_gray_zone_pipeline_stage_doc: GHL stage 'Nurture' (per GHL UI) — used for gray-zone qualifications. Stage name in GHL differs from the 'Manual Review' label used in older code comments; the ID is canonical.
_reject_pipeline_stage_doc: GHL stage 'Closed Lost' — used for pre-qualification rejects (out-of-scope industry, hard exclusions). Same stage unsubscribes already use.
_pause_on_stage_ids_doc: GHL stage IDs (Closed Won + Closed Lost). When an opportunity moves into these, the bidirectional sync pauses the contact's Smartlead lead so no more follow-ups go out.

business_context._doc_status: Most fields populated 2026-05-20 with JT_DRAFT_* values, awaiting Shawn confirmation per docs/client_communications/2026-05-20_shawn_business_context_request.md. Website + Shawn email are real values; phone + address still TBD. booking_link remains PLACEHOLDER until JT flips it post-launch readiness. credential_mentions all marked AWAITING_SHAWN_CONFIRM — responder should NOT surface any credential until the AWAITING_ prefix is removed for that specific item.
business_context.tagline: JT_DRAFT_2026-05-20 — Twin Cities owner-operated commercial cleaning — built for facility managers who care about consistency.
business_context.service_area: Minneapolis-St. Paul metro, 45-minute radius from downtown Minneapolis or downtown St. Paul
services_offered: Medical office cleaning (medical practitioner offices only — not hospitals, not dentist offices)
value_props: Specialized auto-scrubber equipment for hard-floor properties
Twin Cities owner-operated — not a national chain
50%+ margin discipline keeps quality high (won't race to the bottom on price)
Responsive within the 45-min Minneapolis–St. Paul metro radius
Route-density pricing for clusters of nearby properties
business_context.pricing_response: Pricing depends on square footage, frequency, and what kind of facility you're running — happy to put a number together after a quick 10-minute walkthrough so I can see the space.
business_context.scheduling_info: We schedule walkthroughs within the week, usually mornings or after-hours so we don't disrupt your team.
business_context.sales_process: Quick walkthrough call → on-site visit → written proposal within 48 hours → start date typically within 2 weeks of signing.

common_objections._doc: Pre-written response templates the contextual responder uses verbatim or paraphrases. JT_DRAFT values approved 2026-05-20; pending Shawn confirmation.
common_objections.already_have_vendor: Totally understood — most folks we walk through with already have someone. Worth a 10-minute call just so we're on your bench if anything changes? Plenty of property managers we work with had a long-term vendor before they switched.
common_objections.send_pricing_first: Happy to put something together, but pricing for facility cleaning really hinges on a quick walkthrough — square footage and what's actually in the space drive everything. 10 minutes on-site lets me put a real number in front of you rather than a guess.
common_objections.not_in_budget: No problem. If anything shifts in the next quarter, would it be useful if I sent a quick check-in note in 60 days? Won't pester.
common_objections.too_busy_now: Hear you. When does the dust settle — end of Q3 or sooner? I'll put a note on my side to circle back then.
common_objections.contract_locked_in: When does that contract come up for renewal? Worth a walkthrough 60-90 days ahead so you have a real alternative on the table.
